title: dalbergia tricolor
intro: Dalbergia tricolor, also known as the three-colored dalbergia or three-colored rosewood, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae. The medicinal properties of Dalbergia tricolor are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are known for their anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of this plant include the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. In traditional medicine, Dalbergia tricolor is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its herbal preparations may also involve the use of essential oils extracted from its bark, leaves, or roots.
